notes: A context value is supplied only through the explicit context channel; ordinary arguments never satisfy it implicitly. It is reusable, Shareable, no-drop, resource-free, authority-free, and remains part of function identity/override compatibility. The context marker is not a first-class value and grants no ambient lookup.
Current law: generalized context/AOP/provider anchors remain D-MAD, not ordinary source. NumericArray context anchor is the only current stable `&` surface.
Current trace-bounded responsibility for Requires/ensures contract clauses. No authority, ownership, effect, witness, or source activation is implied beyond the exact current references.
return/ret/break/continue/throw may carry pure if/!if guard clauses in their own statement context.

Historical rejection identity only. Deeplus does not admit arbitrary user-declared glyphs, fixity, precedence, associativity, or dispatch tables, including in Preview design. Bounded Recovery may recognize an old declaration solely to emit CUSTOM_OPERATOR_DECLARATION_NOT_CURRENT with zero admitted AST/HIR/MIR/API residue. Named Trait methods, functions, messages, and APIs remain the general extension path. This rejection is distinct from the Stable exact binary +,-,* Add/Subtract/Multiply fixed-glyph conformance profile.
downgrade_reason: Arbitrary custom glyph, fixity, precedence, associativity, and dispatch declarations are rejected rather than retained as Preview design.

notes: Repeated positional capture and unfold, exact static labels, one final Record-backed named-rest collector, and exact function/API rest residue form one closed call-shape law. Record labels are static; Map keys are runtime values and cannot feed named arguments. The direct design owners are StaticCallShapeAdmitted, NamedRestCollectorAdmitted, NamedRestParameterRecordAndLastPosition, and FunctionRestResiduePreserved.

The opener is followed by newline, the closer is on its own line, and the longest exact common indentation prefix is stripped. Product execution remains NOT_RUN.
Verifier scope expanded for feature-gate diagnostics, grammar profile closure, example block outcomes, parser receipt coverage, and map-unfold migration drift.

`value!!{...}` performs deep same-type derivation by a finite graph traversal with an identity map: cycles terminate, shared subgraphs stay shared, authority/failure are explicit, and partial results are rolled back and cleaned on failure. Product support NOT_RUN.
`CleanupDecl` owns the sole current lifecycle declaration spelling, `def#cleanup()`. The nonterminal name `DropDecl` is provenance-only and has no current grammar route.
A deferred invocation reserves moved/cleanup-owned places until scope exit and forbids hidden clone, rebind, move, or suspension.

For one full expression, evaluate operations in source order; the first escaping failure is primary. Cleanup runs in reverse acquisition order. Later cleanup failures are appended to the primary failure's suppressed list in cleanup execution order and never replace it.

notes: Current law: method dispatch/witness markers are explicit: `.` final, `+` open, `*.` override-final, `*+` override-open. Marker is declaration metadata, not part of selector identity.

Every instance method in a class-like owner has exactly one class dispatch marker `.`, `+`, `*.`, or `*+`. Trait methods use the same glyphs through a distinct TraitWitnessMarker AST domain. Stored fields and non-method associated requirements never carry these markers.
Doc line comments start with //! and doc block comments start with //!! and end with !!//.

Current trace-bounded responsibility for Removal of dollar declaration/member/flow-binding sigils. No authority, ownership, effect, witness, or source activation is implied beyond the exact current references.

QualifiedPath contains one or more Identifier segments, with adjacent segments joined by `::`. The same static separator is reused by the exact type-side, associated, qualified-extension, and full-enum-case owners.

Stable built-in pointwise logical family for &&, ||, ^^, and prefix ~~. The historical packed route is unchanged: one exact normalized known-width integer or one identical finite bitfield/#flags domain produces the same domain. The shaped route additionally admits only exact same-shape NumericArray operands whose elements have one exact known-width integer domain, and produces that same shape and element domain. Binary operands are evaluated left-to-right exactly once; there is no short circuit, flow narrowing, implicit broadcasting, width/signedness/element conversion, user conformance lookup, or runtime fallback. Scalar Bool remains rejected; NumericArray<Bool>, dynamic or heterogeneous shapes, generic collections, and user-defined carriers remain deferred or rejected. Product support remains NOT_RUN.
